name: [OPS] Restore workflows from GitHub to n8n
description: Automated workflow: [OPS] Restore workflows from GitHub to n8n. This workflow integrates 8 different services: stickyNote, n8n, merge, set, manualTrigger. It contains 17 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

parameters.content:
## Workflow - Restore Backups
This workflow will restore backed-up workflows from Github. 
It is launch by testing the workflow

### Setup
Open Globals and update the values below
**repo.owner:** This is your Github username
**repo.name:** This is the name of your repository
**repo.path:** This is the folder where your workflows are saved, within the repository.

If your username was `n8n-io` and your repository was called `n8n-backups` and you wanted the workflows to go into a `workflows` folder you would set:

repo.owner - n8n-io
repo.name - n8n-backups
repo.path - workflows

parameters.content:
## Get all Github files
1. List all the files from your repository
2. Get each file as a JSON. 
3. The content, retrieved as base64, is converted in the "Set Name and Content" node


The "Set Name and Content" node creates a list of workflows with name and content, in order to compare it with the existing n8n workflows in the workspace.

parameters.content:
## Workflow - Restore Backups
This workflow will restore backed-up workflows from Github. 
It is launch by testing the workflow

### Setup
Open Globals and update the values below
**repo.owner:** This is your Github username
**repo.name:** This is the name of your repository
**repo.path:** This is the folder where your workflows are saved, within the repository.

If your username was `n8n-io` and your repository was called `n8n-backups` and you wanted the workflows to go into a `workflows` folder you would set:

repo.owner - n8n-io
repo.name - n8n-backups
repo.path - workflows

## Get all Github files
1. List all the files from your repository
2. Get each file as a JSON. 
3. The content, retrieved as base64, is converted in the "Set Name and Content" node


The "Set Name and Content" node creates a list of workflows with name and content, in order to compare it with the existing n8n workflows in the workspace.

## Merge Github and n8n workflows

Here, we only keep the workflows from Github that doesn't already exist in n8n workspace, based on the name

## Create n8n workflow

Create the n8n workflow using:
- Content saved in Github
- Name of the file in Github


If the workflow name already exist in n8n, then the workflow is not created - to avoid duplicates.

## Get existing n8n workflows

1. Get all workflows
2. Prepare a list of workflows in order to compare it with the workflows saved in the Github repo.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "On clicking 'execute'".
Open the "Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Workflow name already exists" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If workflow already exists" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "GitHub - get all files" node (n8n-nodes-base.github) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "n8n - get all workflows" node (n8n-nodes-base.n8n) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "GitHub - get each file" node (n8n-nodes-base.github) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "n8n - create workflow" node (n8n-nodes-base.n8n) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge globals and files" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge Github and n8n workflows - Keep only non existing workflows based on the name" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Globals".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set n8n existing workflows names".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set name and content".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
